issue_title: UnicodeError Gunicorn generate_latest multiprocess
issue_body:
Hi all,

We are currently seeing the following error from our prometheus endpoint:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/cats/server/app_log.py", line 37, in _apply_logging
    result = fn(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/cats/server/prometheus_views.py", line 14, in prometheus_view
    output = generate_latest(registry)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/exposition.py", line 63, in generate_latest
    for metric in registry.collect():
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/core.py", line 97, in collect
    for metric in collector.collect():
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/multiprocess.py", line 24, in collect
    d = core._MmapedDict(f)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/core.py", line 335, in __init__
    for key, _, pos in self._read_all_values():
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/core.py", line 365, in _read_all_values
    yield encoded.decode('utf-8'), value, pos
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xa0 in position 102: invalid start byte
```

Weirdly, at first the endpoint responds with our metrics as expected, then after a short period of time as requests come in we start to get the above.  We are finding it impossible to debug what character is causing the issue.  

We only have two metrics:

```
METRICS['application_latency'] = Histogram('application_latency',
                                           "Metrics for application latency",
                                           ['resourceName', 'method', 'worker'])



METRICS['application_responses'] = Counter(
    'application_responses',
    'Metrics for application responses per endpoint, method and http response code',
    ['resourceName', 'method', 'status', 'worker'])
```

And increment them like so:

```
        METRICS['application_responses'].labels(resourceName=resource_name, method=method, status=status_code,
                                                worker=str(os.getpid())).inc()

        METRICS['application_latency'].labels(resourceName=resource_name, method=method,
                                              worker=str(os.getpid())).observe(response_time)
```

All the labels are just strings and the most complex is resourceName which looks like "service.my_method" for example.  Any pointers on how we could possibly diagnose this would be great as currently we are completely stuck.  
We have followed the gunicorn multiprocess example exactly.

Our gunicorn conf is:

```
PROMETHEUS_METRICS = '/prometheus-metrics'

bind = '0.0.0.0:9077'
worker_class = 'gevent'
workers = 8
worker_connections = 8000
worker_tmp_dir = '/worker-tmp'
backlog = 16384
keepalive = 8


def on_starting(_server):
    [os.remove("{}/{}".format(PROMETHEUS_METRICS, f)) for f in os.listdir(PROMETHEUS_METRICS)]

def child_exit(_server, worker):
    from prometheus_client import multiprocess
    multiprocess.mark_process_dead(worker.pid)
```

We also set this environment variable before starting:

```
prometheus_multiproc_dir=/prometheus-metrics
```

Finally, we are running kubernetes hosted on AWS.

issue_title: Windows version conditions do not apply for Windows 10
issue_body:
Some artifacts have conditions in the form of `os_major_version >= X AND os_minor_version >= Y`.
This fails starting with Windows 10, which can have version 10.0. A condition like `os_major_version >= 6 AND os_minor_version >= 1` will fail here although the artifact applies.

One example is
https://github.com/ForensicArtifacts/artifacts/blob/015b37543d97ad719f92d51b0a6cd548e4b9dab6/data/windows.yaml#L1335

I can think of multiple solutions to this:

- More complicated conditions like  `(os_major_version >= 6 AND os_minor_version >= 1) OR os_major version >= 7`
- A semantic version compare, something like `os_version >= "6.1"`

issue_title: set up CI
Addresses in Texas Like '# Country Road 212' should be parsed differently
issue_body:
So I am attempting to parse the address: "1 COUNTRY ROAD 212"
Which is giving the following output: (note I am parsing it into JSON format) 
```JSON
[{"AddressNumber": "1"}, {"StreetName": "COUNTRY"}, {"StreetNamePostType": "ROAD"}, {"OccupancyIdentifier": "212"}]
```

When in Texas it uses the `Country Road #` as a street name for the rural areas.
Output should be something like:
```JSON
[{"AddressNumber": "1"}, {"StreetName": "COUNTRY ROAD 212"}]
```

I assume that something `Country Rd 212` should be parsed with the same idea as above.


This is kind of a corner case, and I do have a work around for it, in my code, but I thought I should post an issue report anyways :+1:

issue_title: ssl.PROTOCOL_TLSv1 problems
issue_body:
While testing uw_restclients_r25 with the 25Live web service, I'm now getting the error "[SSL: TLSV1_ALERT_PROTOCOL_VERSION] tlsv1 alert protocol version (_ssl.c:590)"
I don't believe I changed anything on my end since it was last working, so I think this means they just updated their servers to reject TLSv1, which restclients_core.dao has hard-coded. issue_title: MVP: auto-resume for content importing from network
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

We've repeatedly observed that importing content over a flaky internet connection is a frustrating process because every time a connection times out or otherwise fails, the process needs to be restarted from scratch.

### Expected behavior

If an import fails partway through during the content download phase, the import system should not immediately stop the job and report the import has having failed.

Instead, import command should remain active and try to resume importing where it left off for some period of time, before finally ending and reporting status as failed.

A possible implementation might look something like:

* If an import fails due to timeout or lost connection during the download phase
  * delete any partially-downloaded files if necessary
  * into an 'attempting resume' mode
  * every 5 minutes try to resume
  * if no new data is downloaded after 1 day, report a failure

This change is simply an MVP to make it a bit more viable to download large channels over flaky connections, and does not involve any changes to the UI.

### User-facing consequences

Without this change, users on flaky connections have very little chance of successfully downloading a large channel.

issue_title: detect and warn about unsupported browsers
issue_labels: [{'id': 492821376, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20frontend', 'name': 'DEV: frontend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 667034570, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/P0', 'name': 'P0', 'color': 'EB5A46', 'default': False}, {'id': 492818262, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TASK:%20new%20feature', 'name': 'TASK: new feature', 'color': '72486f', 'default': False}, {'id': 750739673, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/user-text', 'name': 'user-text', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
There are a few browsers that we explicitly do not support. Currently, many of these browsers leave the user with a "broken" experience - for example a loading screen that never goes away.

Instead, we should detect browsers that are not supported and show an explicit message with instructions about what to do.

For example, if we detect that the user is on IE10, we should instruct them to use Firefox. The instructions might vary depending on detected OS (e.g. unsupported browsers on Android might have different recommended next-steps)

We should also update the [`browserlist`](https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/blob/develop/browserslist) file to accurately reflect our supported browsers.

nice-to-have: also detect when javascript is turned off and warn about that, too

issue_title: Handler misbehaviour
issue_body:
Hi Mangopay folks,

After scratching my head a few hours by digging in your code, I came to the conclusion that the way you implemented the APIRequest handler is not working.

It looks like your implementation completely ignores custom APIRequest like the one below because it relies on the get_default_handler() method only. 
```
from mangopay.api import APIRequest

handler = APIRequest(sandbox=True, timeout=60.0)
```

This small POC below enlights this fact by showing the references of the registered handler.

```
import mangopay

from mangopay.api import APIRequest
from mangopay.resources import Dispute

mangopay.client_id='your-client-id'
mangopay.passphrase='your-passphrase'

handler = APIRequest(timeout=4)

dispute = Dispute()

print(dispute.handler)
print(mangopay.get_default_handler())

print(handler)
```
As you can see after executing it, it will output something like that : 
```
<mangopay.api.APIRequest object at 0x7f4deae39b70>                                                                                                                     
<mangopay.api.APIRequest object at 0x7f4deae39b70>                                                                    
<mangopay.api.APIRequest object at 0x7f4def926d68> 
```
By looking at the references of the objects, we can easily understand that the resource is using the memoized handler and not the custom one.

As a consequence, defining things like sandbox, timeout, proxies, storage_strategy or anything in the APIRequest constructor does not work at all.

issue_title: Search queries are not URL encoded
issue_body:
If I try to run a search against `/api/v1/search/pulses` with spaces in the query, I get a Bad Request:

```python
>>> otx.search_pulses('phpMyAdmin honeypot logs for 2016-10-01')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "OTXv2.py", line 293, in search_pulses
    return self._get_paginated_resource(search_pulses_url, max_results=max_results)
  File "OTXv2.py", line 317, in _get_paginated_resource
    json_data = self.get(next_page_url)
  File "OTXv2.py", line 79, in get
    raise BadRequest("Bad Request")

OTXv2.BadRequest: 'Bad Request'
```

This is because the query in the `search_pulses` method is not URL encoded, so it tries to GET something like this: `https://otx.alienvault.com/api/v1/search/pulses?q=phpMyAdmin honeypot logs for 2016-10-01&`.

To fix this we can use `urllib.quote_plus` for Python 2 (or `urllib.parse.quote_plus()` for Python 3) to properly encode our query.

```python
>>> import urllib
>>> urllib.quote_plus('phpMyAdmin honeypot logs for 2016-10-01')
'phpMyAdmin+honeypot+logs+for+2016-10-01'
```

So a quick fix would be to update the `q=query` in the `search_pulses` method to `q=urllib.quote_plus(q)`, like so:

```python
def search_pulses(self, query, max_results=25):
        search_pulses_url = self.create_url(SEARCH_PULSES, q=urllib.quote_plus(q), page=1, limit=20)
        return self._get_paginated_resource(search_pulses_url, max_results=max_results)

I'm not sure if this is the norm in APIs, but wouldn't it make sense to update your python API module to do the URL encoding? If you're expecting the user to do it, then maybe mention that under the API module documentation?

issue_title: Incorrectly calculated note offsets (due to wrong duration of full-measure rests?)
issue_body: I have a bunch of MusicXML files (exported from Sibelius) containing two-part vocal music. See following image for an example. I'd like to calculate the offset of notes relative the beginning of the piece. Unfortunately, this gives wrong results for notes in the lower part, presumably because the durations of full-measure rests are calculated incorrectly. Example: the offset of the first note in measure 4 should be 43.0 because the previous three measures have the following quarter durations: 19 + 12 + 12 = 43. However, music21 reports the offset of that note as 35.0: ``` >>> import music21 >>> s = music21.converter.parseFile('sample_piece.xml') >>> tenor = s.parts[1] >>> second_tenor_note = tenor.flat.notes[1] >>> print(second_tenor_note.getOffsetInHierarchy(s)) 35.0 ``` Inspecting the .xml file, my best guess is that this happens because the second and third measure are notated using a rest of type 'breve', so the quarter duration of these rests is assumed to be 8. However, since they are full-measure rests their duration should be the duration of the measure itself, which is 12 in both cases. Here is the relevant part of the .xml file: ``` [...] 24 8 9216 1 breve 9216 1 breve [...] ``` Is this a bug in `music21`, or is there something wrong with the exported MusicXML format? Is there currently any workaround for computing the correct note offsets? Many thanks for any pointers!

issue_title: compound issue: deis-database and deis-store-daemon
issue_body:
Was discussing this issue today on slack, this is kind of complex, my deployment is v1.13.2

I have a 5-node cluster and one of my nodes was randomly assigned as controller, store-gateway, database, builder, router, and logger.  I install SNMP on all of my nodes to keep an eye on disk usage, and this node was filling up its disk and had reached about 84% of 80GB.  I've had this happen to me a couple of times and I haven't made a specific set of instructions to repro, but standing up a cluster with whatever all I usually do and waiting about 2 weeks to a month seems to do it reliably.

I looked into why this was happening, and there were two reasons I could see, (1) snmpd service is spamming /var/lib/deis/store/logs/snmpd.log with about 200 lines of text per minute, each time nagios polls it.  This only generated about 500MB of cruft though, (2) much larger deis-store-daemon spam about 10 lines of text per second had generated about 2.5GB of cruft, errors matching this issue from way back: #3969 

These errors from store-daemon didn't look great, I also see now in hindsight that it looks like Ceph is reporting `pool .rgw.buckets has too few pgs`, and maybe this is the root of my problem.  But the overall usage of storage by GB stayed under half of the total 597GB available, and ceph seemed to be operating.


In the interest of getting my cluster back in good working order, I manually stopped (with systemctl) all of the deis services on that node, destroyed their containers using `docker ps` and `docker rm`, deleted all of the images that did not still belong to some running container, and purged all of the dangling volumes.  I was thinking this followed by a reboot would let fleet put those services onto another node, and my database would restore from WAL.

It didn't.  The logs from just before and after what I'm describing looked like this:

[gist: deis-database.log](https://gist.github.com/yebyen/802a2122d6a02134b0001b8723afa1aa)

16:05:44UTC is where fleet started the recovery, on about [line 3797](https://gist.github.com/yebyen/802a2122d6a02134b0001b8723afa1aa#file-deis-database-log-L3797)

You can see before there, a long span of repeating errors ending up in messages like:

```
2016-07-20 15:53:09 UTC LOG:  archive command failed with exit code 2
2016-07-20 15:53:09 UTC DETAIL:  The failed archive command was: envdir /etc/wal-e.d/env wal-e wal-push pg_xlog/000000010000000200000034
2016-07-20 15:53:09 UTC WARNING:  archiving transaction log file "000000010000000200000034" failed too many times, will try again later
```

I have the whole log un-truncated, in case there might be relevant information further back.  I just posted 4000 line snippet because it's quite long.

Anyway, I downed the node and let it reboot, watched all of the services get restarted in a pretty orderly way by fleet, controller came back and started running migrations (...), and I started to suspect that I had lost my deis database for good.  I used deisctl to `set registrationMode` back to enabled, created myself a new admin user, found my app's web processes still up, still routed and healthy, but no longer listed by `deis ps:list` at all, and it looks like I have a (mostly) brand new, healthy cluster.

So, this issue is for postmortem and LTS supporting purposes.  If there was a safer or smarter way to regenerate my database (`deisctl stop database && deisctl uninstall database` then `deisctl install database && deisctl start database`), or if there was some way for me to avoid or detect my WAL archiving failures so I could avoid destroying the database (I think it was still working), or or or...

There is actually probably more than one separate issue documented here, or in the best case maybe it's just one issue that follows from another fault further up the stack, with a root cause.  This is not the first time I've had a cluster blow up like this, in fact I'd say it's probably happened every time with enough patience.

I haven't spent a lot of time writing automated checks for Deis platform health, usually I let check_mk set up a couple of checks for issues that will affect the whole node, problems I can detect by SNMP in a container (node is running out of disk, node is spinning CPU at 100%, out-of-memory, just the basics) and I add a health check to confirm that my app stays up, and leave it at that.  So when minor things start to go wrong (like for some reason WAL can't archive anymore) and they start spamming the logs with errors, they are silently ignored until they are affecting an attempted app deployment or until they are somehow or other becoming more serious problems.

As an aside, if I could have a wish-list item to add to the LTS feature-set, even if it was something built as a separate product or as an add-on (LTS not supposed to get any new features), I would be asking for full-stack health monitoring of Deis platform services.  Something to tell me if WAL logs are current or if they are falling behind, if Ceph is HEALTH_OK or some other status, if one of my MDS is down and needs to be restarted, or if controller has stopped communicating with database.

I can find out when a lot of this stuff is wrong by tailing logs and journals, and I could probably use logstash to set up alerting based on those logs.  For this information to have a central outlet inside of the Deis platform seems like a logical extension though.  Self-healing is absolutely good, but I think it's probably not the be-all end-all of health monitoring.

My first attempt at preventing this from happening again is going to be silencing the logger spam from SNMPD.  SNMP daemon has a quiet mode.  Maybe this wouldn't happen or wouldn't happen so soon if that log spam weren't coming in constantly.

(So from a debugging perspective, I think spamming Ceph's posix store might be the key step to being able to reproduce this problem... I'm not doing very much else out of ordinary to my clusters that could possibly explain why some people don't have this problem)

issue_title: Ceph reports "too many PGs per OSD" in Deis 1.6
issue_body:
In testing Deis 1.6, my cluster reports:


This seems to be a new warning in the Hammer release of Ceph, which we're shipping in Deis 1.6. This got me thinking - perhaps we aren't calculating placement group usage properly, and this is contributing to the various issues we're seeing with Ceph performance (particularly on platform start).

I posted a message to the Ceph mailing list asking for clarification: http://lists.ceph.com/pipermail/ceph-users-ceph.com/2015-May/001222.html

It seems that the placement group documentation is unclear. If we should shoot for 300 PGs per OSD, then it seems like we should use a `pg_num` of 16, since even 32 would result in more than 300 per OSD.

12 pools * 32 = 384
12 pools * 16 = 192

However, if this computation is based on the resources of the host, it'll be harder to choose a default value.

issue_title: Continued host clock skew issues
issue_body:
We tried to solve clock skew in #3300 by adding time synchronization to the cloud-config script, but this seems to have not done a whole lot to remedy the issue - users are still frequently reporting that their hosts are going out of sync (anecdotally, this seems to happen more often on EC2?).

We really need the expertise of our community to help us come up with a solution for continually syncing clocks via NTP.

issue_title: Add SIGHASH opcode
issue_body:
### What is wrong?
Checking transaction signatures in account abstraction (#225) involves calculating the hash of the transaction, excluding the `data` field containing the signature itself. This is not possible right now, as there are no opcodes for all transaction fields.

### How can it be fixed

Add an opcode `SIGHASH` with byte value `0x3f` that gets `sha3(rlp(tx \ {data}))`, where `tx \ {data}` refers to the transaction without the data field.

issue_title: Filters that don't rely on server state
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

Filters suck.

- Sometimes the server loses the `filter_id`
- You don't really have any clarity to what is actually happening under the hood on the server.
- You can't use filters with infura
- They don't work if you're load balanced across multiple nodes.

### How can it be fixed?

I propose we implement a configurable *filter backend* engine.  For now, the default engine will retain the current functionality of using the node to handle filters.

However, we also implement a new local filter backend that looks something like the following.

```python
from cytoolz import (
    merge,
    sliding_window,
)


def log_filter(web3, filter_params, page_size=10):
    # TODO: handle pending/latest/earliest and cases where these are not included in the filter params.
    from_block = filter_params['fromBlock']
    to_block = filter_params['toBlock']

    # TODO: handle dynamic block ranges to enforce reasonable request sizes.
    for block_number in range(from_block, to_block + 1):
        params = merge(filter_params, {'fromBlock': block_number, 'toBlock': block_number})
        logs = web3.eth.getLogs(params)

        for page in sliding_window(page_size, logs):
            yield page
```

This `log_filter` generator can be used under the hood in place of using the node based filters to handle all filter logic locally, only relying on the node for `eth_getLogs` which is stateless.  Each time a new filter is created, the filter backend will create one of these generators for it and keep track of it internally.  Then, any calls to `eth_getFilterChanges` can just return the `next(...)` for the appropriate filter generator.  We *should* be able to do something similar with `eth_getFilterLogs` by re-generating the original filter generator and then returning all of the values up-to-the-current state of the generator.

issue_title: Creating environments in Anaconda duplicates icons to all applications
issue_body:
Machine:  Windows 7 w/ 16 Gigs RAM
Anaconda Version:  saw this in 4.1, but it is more pervasive in 4.2

What happens:  Use anaconda commands to either create new environments or add nontebook kernels to your Jupyter menus (through command line or through GUI for environment creation) and it creates a full set of Anaconda icons for each environment.  However, only root environment is hooked to these applications, so these are just ghosts.  Some of the icons crash when you load them.  Others are just renamed versions of what you already have.

Add environment with the GUI and this happens every time

From command line, it usually happens when you add a Notebook Kernel menu for an environment but sometimes happens during creation as well.

I was running tests on a broken environment trying to shake some stuff out before I will blow it away and re-install fresh to get it right.  These screenshots show this symptom:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/23440318/21624582/b498981c-d1d5-11e6-859d-7a1559d4a456.png)

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/23440318/21624605/c5fdab24-d1d5-11e6-9c89-996c43206fc1.png)

(root) C:\Users\Mitch>python -m ipykernel install --user --name Py2tst2 --displa
y-name "Python (Py2tst2)"
Installed kernelspec Py2tst2 in C:\Users\Mitch\AppData\Roaming\jupyter\kernels\p
y2tst2

(root) C:\Users\Mitch>python -m ipykernel install --user --name Py3tst --display
-name "Python (Py3tst)"

Installed kernelspec Py3tst in C:\Users\Mitch\AppData\Roaming\jupyter\kernels\py
3tst

issue_title: Miniconda-latest-* (without python version) have been removed
issue_body:
The installer seems to be disappeared not long ago (I see failing builds for one of the projects on appveyor for a week now, but some other projects are still fine, presumably due to caching) and now only the Miniconda2-* and Miniconda3-* files are available for windows. https://github.com/astropy/ci-helpers/issues/159
According to the footnote at https://repo.continuum.io/miniconda/ they should be still around.

I'm updating our ci-helpers to use Miniconda3* from now, but having an issue might be useful to save others from running into the same thing.

issue_title: Simple Install Issue PLEASE HELP A BEGINNER!
issue_body:
Hi Coders,

This one should be easy for ya.

I'm here trying to use this 64-bit installer to install Anaconda 4.3.1 on my 64-bit Windows computer. I'm going through the prompts that they give me but am unable to pass a certain point without an error message popping up, and have been stuck here for quite a little while.

It happens when I try to choose an install location. As soon as I try to put the program in the recommended folder (C:\Users\Me\Anaconda3), the following error message shows up:

**The installation directory has 1 space. This can cause problems with several Conda packages. Please consider removing the space.**

Now I've tried putting it in my Program Files folder, and also my Program Files (x86). I've tried creating new folders in different locations and it's still not budging smoothly. Instead, this message shows up:

**Directory 'C:\Program Files' is not empty, please choose a different location.**

 I don't wanna shoehorn this, as that would likely cause some annoying problems in the future. Any advice for someone just trying to take a class?

Thanks!

issue_title: Local `file://` channels for `conda-build` added?
issue_body: When adding other channels to my `.condarc`, I'm find that `conda` is adding `file://` channels for `conda-build` to the `.condarc`. This is problematic as these directories don't exist yet. As a result, `conda` is unable to work afterwards. Here's an [example log](https://circleci.com/gh/nanshe-org/docker_nanshe/6?utm_campaign=vcs-integration-link&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github-build-link) showing this failure.

issue_title: Unable to install conda package from root of drive
issue_body:
On Windows it's not possible to install a local package (from .tar.bz2) if the package is located in the root of a drive. This is probably an error related to how the package name is separated from the folder on Windows. See full traceback below. You can see it's trying to create a folder beginning with ":".

The work-around is to move the package to folder somewhere else.

```
(python36) D:\>conda install glpk-4.61-py36_0.tar.bz2

conda GitHub issue tracker at:

    https://github.com/conda/conda/issues



Current conda install:

issue_title: conda install 'setuptools<8' doesn't install the right version
issue_body:
```
$conda create -n test 'setuptools<=8' --dry
Fetching package metadata: ..............................
Solving package specifications: .............
Package plan for installation in environment /Users/aaronmeurer/anaconda/envs/test:

The following packages will be downloaded:

    package                    |            build
    ---------------------------|-----------------
    setuptools-15.2            |           py34_0         441 KB  defaults

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    ncurses:    5.9-0        https://conda.binstar.org/asmeurer/osx-64/
    openssl:    1.0.1k-1     defaults
    pip:        6.1.1-py34_0 defaults
    python:     3.4.3-0      defaults
    readline:   6.2.5-1      https://conda.binstar.org/asmeurer/osx-64/
    setuptools: 15.2-py34_0  defaults
    sqlite:     3.8.4.1-1    defaults
    tk:         8.5.18-0     defaults
    xz:         5.0.5-0      defaults
    zlib:       1.2.8-0      defaults

Dry run: exiting
```

The reason is that verlib doesn't recognize `8` as a valid version, so it falls back to string comparison. It looks like the PEP 440 code at https://github.com/pypa/packaging/blob/master/packaging/version.py does this right. 

See also https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1107 and https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/915.

issue_title: Conda env spontaneously removed :confused:
issue_body:
Hi,

we are running conda 4.2.11 on a Scientific Linux cluster

Recently we've experienced some strange issues where an entire conda environment suddenly disappears, leaving a bin folder with only some links; `activate`, `deactivate`, and `conda` left in it.

What we've observed is that it seems to do with many `source activate [env]` commands being executed at the same time.

Please let me know if I can provide any other information to aid in debugging the issue

issue_title: Creating new environment with conda 4.4.7 surprisingly upgrades Python from 2.7 to 3.6
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior When creating a new Python environment with conda 4.4.7, Python is upgraded from 2.7 to 3.6 unexpectedly. Is this new behavior intentional in 4.4.x? ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Run Miniconda OS X installer (though I see same behavior on Linux) to say, `$HOME/env` 2. Run `conda update conda` 3. `export PATH=$HOME/env/bin:$PATH` --- I know `conda activate base` is recommended but see #6739. 4. Just to show my Python version: ``` $ python --version Python 2.7.14 :: Anaconda, Inc. ``` 5. Then try to create an env with just numpy, and see that it wants to create it with Python 3.6.4, which is unexpected to me. If I don't do step 2 (keep conda at 4.3.1) above, it behaves as I expect it should. issue_title: `conda list` doesn't have column headers
issue_body: The `conda list` command outputs 3 columns of data for which there are no column headers. The first appears to be `package name`, the second `version`, the third is possibly `feature+build number`? It would be great to have these headers added and could be done so in the already hash-prefixed lines so as not to upset filters/pipes relying on the package list format. Thanks.

issue_title: sqlite3 in Python 3 should have enable_load_extension

issue_title: different sqlite versions installed on Linux vs OS X
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [X] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior When I install the same conda environment, I get two different versions of sqlite on Linux vs OS X ### Steps to Reproduce ``` curl -O https://repo.continuum.io/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-MacOSX-x86_64.sh bash Miniconda3-latest-MacOSX-x86_64.sh -b -p i-1 source i-1/etc/profile.d/conda.sh conda activate base curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cgat-developers/cgat-apps/master/conda/environments/apps-production.yml conda update --all --yes conda env create --name test --file apps-production.yml conda activate test conda list sqlite ``` On Linux, `conda list sqlite` returns `sqlite-3.20.1-2`. On OS X, `conda list sqlite` returns `sqlite-3.20.1-0`. `sqlite-3.20.1-0` on OS X does not work, returning: ``` dyld: Library not loaded: @rpath/libtinfo.5.dylib Referenced from: /Users/sebastian/conda/i-1/envs/test/bin/sqlite3 Reason: image not found Abort trap: 6 ``` ## Expected Behavior `conda list sqlite` should install `sqlite-3.20.1-2` on both Linux and OS X. ## Environment Information

issue_title: conda packages dependencies: lack of update, problems after update
issue_body:
Hi,

We have a channel where we distribute our packages (https://anaconda.org/acellera). We find conda a great platform to distribute our software, but we find the updates rather troublesome much of the times, specially related with dependencies.

**Issue number 1:** When doing `conda update htmd -c acellera`, our main package, we notice many times that the dependencies are not updated. We know there is a flag, `--update-dependencies`, specifically related with this, but we trust the default to be working. Is this not the case? In this end, we sometimes recommend people to use the `conda update --all`, which I've read somewhere that it should be used carefully. We currently also implemented a way of having specific versions associated with the package instead of keeping it to the latest, but it doesn't seem like it has improved the behaviour much. 

**Issue number 1:** Alternatively to the behaviour in Issue 1, the package is updated, as shown by `conda list`, but the Python module still behaves like the old one, suggesting that there may be a conflict between the older installation and the new one.

**Issue number 3:** Sometimes, we change our package, and create a new dependency for it. For new installations, everything works fine, but for installations that are updated to a new version using `conda update`, for some people and more often than we wished for, the package cannot de imported, like it was missing. Despite that, the command `conda list` reports it.

Many times, we are left clueless about these issues, and all we can suggest to our users is to reinstall conda, which normally fixes the issues. Do all these issues mean that `conda update` cannot be trusted? Asking our users to always be installing conda is very annoying.

We appreciate any feedback that could improve this issues.

João

Yuanhua

issue_title: Cobol Project Support
issue_body:
I am opening this issue to discuss the possibility to add project support to the IDE.

OpenCobolIDE has been designed to work on a single file basis. You open a file and compile it.  Well, this is not entirely true, actually there is a sort of implicit project support: the IDE will first parse the file you want to compile to detect its dependencies and compile them before compiling the file itself.

Not having a project concept makes the IDE very easy to use for a beginner (there is no makefile, not config to setup) but can be very limitating for the experts.

Introducing the concept of project would bring the following benefits:
 - ability to specify which file should be compiled and how. With the current design, the compiler properties are applied globally on every file that get compiled.
 - ability to add VCS support (#83)

A very important point to consider when implementing this feature is **that it is very important to keep the ability to open and compile a single file.** This means that we must have **two different kind of windows**: a single document window that you will use to work on a single unit (mainly for the beginners) and a project view with vcs integration and all the bells and whistles a project can offer. **This means a complete redesign of the application!**

Also if we implement project support, **we need to find a build system for building the project sources** but I am not sure if there is a standard build system for cobol (I don't want a makefile because IMHO this goes against the philosophy of an IDE where you should be able to add new source files without editing anything, using pure GUI actions).

@bofinken @Astalaseven @LuizFlavio @martyheyman @abbhishek Any thoughts on this? What build system are you using for building your cobol projects and what would you like to see in OpenCobolIDE?

issue_title: [Enhancement] Add option to abort the compile
issue_body:
Sometimes the compilation may need too much time (especially if we add additional pre-parsers) or there is something borked. Then "abort compile" would be very helpfull as the current implementation just disables compile/run in this case.

This would be the things to implement

* add an abort function and button
* if compile or run is used enable the abort button
* when clicked: abort started processes (if possible)
* delete intermediate files (if generated/copied by OCIDE and not locked)
* enable compile/run, disable abort

issue_title: Delete templates without an index
issue_body:
Each Beat generates a template for each version of the Beat to make sure each event sent by a Beat gets the right template applied. Beats are updated over time and old indices can be cleaned up by curator. But the old templates stay around. Right now they have to be manually deleted.

It would be great to have a feature in curator that deletes all templates for which no index exists. As Beats creates a template when the first event is sent, it would mean that for all the templates that exist without an index, the indices for it were deleted and not beats send data anymore. 

There could be edge cases where a beat did not send for a longer time but I think this is more an edge case.

Config wise I'm thinking of something like:

```
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_templates
    description: >-
      Delete unused templates tempalte
    options:
      delete_templates_without_index: True
```

issue_title: [KeyVault] Newest CLI versions missing "all" option for az keyvault set-policy command
issue_body:
### Description
In the past we had instructions to use the parameter all when specifying permissions for key/secret:

Currently, that option is not available, so at the moment you need to type all options separated by space.

This is the output of the help on CLI 2.0.9+DEV

   --key-permissions        : Space separated list. Possible values: encrypt, decrypt, wrapKey,
                               unwrapKey, sign, verify, get, list, create, update, import, delete,
                               backup, restore, recover, purge.
    --secret-permissions     : Space separated list. Possible values: get, list, set, delete,
                               backup, restore, recover, purge.

---

### Environment summary

issue_title: [App Service] Help text for creating a deployment slot isn't completely clear
issue_body: If you run `az appservice web deployment slot create -h`, the `--configuration-source` parameter doesn't include a description, and therefore, it might be initially confusing to users what it expects.. Could we add a description that simply mentions this is the name of an existing slot to use as the source of the new slot?

issue_title: [DNS] Could record sets be implicitly created when adding new records?
issue_body:
I may be missing something, but it doesn't seem like the `az network dns record <TYPE> add` commands allow implicitly creating a record set if it doesn't already exist. [This behavior](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/dns/dns-operations-recordsets-cli#create-a-dns-record) existed in the previous xplat CLI, and would help simplify the experience for common cases where there aren't multiple records in a zone with the same type and name.

Right now, you'd have to run `az network dns record-set create`, and then go create your actual record, which feels a little heavy weight for simple scenarios.

issue_title: How do you use resource filters?
issue_body:
I've successfully made a ResourceManagementClient and it can list resources with `client.resources.list()`. I'd like to filter them by type, to e.g. access compute resources. The list method has a `filter` kwarg that looks appropriate, but the docstring/Swagger doesn't explain how to use it.

I've found this document online: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/azure/dn790569.aspx which suggests a format. I've tried both:

```
filter="startswith(resourceType, \"Microsoft.Storage\")"
```

and 

```
filter="startswith(resourceType, Microsoft.Storage)")
```

... and in both cases I get:

```
CloudError: Invalid $filter 'startswith(resourceType, Microsoft.Storage)' specified in the query string.
```

I've also looked at the C# client, but that appears to have a bunch of extra magic so that people don't have to construct strings themselves.

issue_title: Progress callback enhancements
issue_body:
### Which service(blob, file, queue) does this issue concern?
Blob

### What problem was encountered?
create_blob_from_stream :: progress_callback

### Issue

progress callback is currently defined as: **progress_callback(progress, total)**

I would like to add the blob name to this as well. The issue is that if we are asynchronously calling the Batch CLI with a number of files, the callback will only contain the progress and the total to upload. We need the file/blob name in there as well in order to report progress back to the user.

Current data throughput in KB/s would also be super helpful and go a great deal forwards in diagnosing customer issues.

The new signature would be: **progress_callback(progress, total, blob_name, current_throughput)**. adding optional parameters to the end would not affect existing implementations.

What we need is to make calls at block level with encrypted block level data without having to download full file, which is currently not supported by SDK without having to use protected functions from the SDK(To do this, we have to use some protected encryption functions and models). Please provide us a solution for how to upload a mix of small and large files by giving us a call at Block level with CSE and other block level tasks.

issue_title: Adding routes to subapps is broken after attaching subapp to the parent app
issue_body:
## Long story short

If one adds routes to subapp before `app.add_subapp` accessing them works well, otherwise it results in 404.

## Expected behaviour

It's naturally expected that routing will work well regardless of the order of function calls.

## Actual behaviour

Given `sub_app_cbv.py`:
```python
import sys
import aiohttp.web

API_URL_PREFIX = r'/api/v1'

class PingView(aiohttp.web.View):
    async def get(self):
        return aiohttp.web.Response(text='pong')

def just_app_with_routes_def(app, api_app):
    app.router.add_route('GET', API_URL_PREFIX + r'/ping/', PingView)

def sub_app_after_routes_def(app, api_app):
    api_app.router.add_route('GET', r'/ping/', PingView)
    app.add_subapp(API_URL_PREFIX, api_app)

def sub_app_before_routes_def(app, api_app):
    app.add_subapp(API_URL_PREFIX, api_app)
    api_app.router.add_route('GET', r'/ping/', PingView)

def run_app(coro_name):
    print(f'Going to run {coro_name}')
    setup_app = globals()[coro_name]
    app = aiohttp.web.Application()
    api_app = aiohttp.web.Application()
    setup_app(app, api_app)
    aiohttp.web.run_app(app)

def main():
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        raise RuntimeError('Supply one cli argument plz [just_app_with_routes_def|sub_app_after_routes_def|sub_app_before_routes_def]')
    coro_name = sys.argv[1]
    run_app(coro_name)

__name__ == '__main__' and main()
```

##### Demo 1

```shell
$ python sub_app_cbv.py just_app_with_routes_def
Going to run just_app_with_routes_def
======== Running on http://0.0.0.0:8080 ========
(Press CTRL+C to quit)
```

```shell
$ http :8080/api/v1/ping/
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Length: 4
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
Date: Thu, 11 Jan 2018 23:17:52 GMT
Server: Python/3.6 aiohttp/2.3.7
pong
```

##### Demo 2

```shell
$ python sub_app_cbv.py sub_app_after_routes_def
Going to run sub_app_after_routes_def
======== Running on http://0.0.0.0:8080 ========
(Press CTRL+C to quit)
```

```shell
$ http :8080/api/v1/ping/
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Length: 4
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
Date: Thu, 11 Jan 2018 23:20:58 GMT
Server: Python/3.6 aiohttp/2.3.7
pong
```

##### Demo 3

```shell
$ python sub_app_cbv.py sub_app_before_routes_def
Going to run sub_app_before_routes_def
======== Running on http://0.0.0.0:8080 ========
(Press CTRL+C to quit)
```

```shell
$ http :8080/api/v1/ping/
HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
Content-Length: 14
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
Date: Thu, 11 Jan 2018 23:21:41 GMT
Server: Python/3.6 aiohttp/2.3.7
404: Not Found
```

## Steps to reproduce

See above.

## Your environment

Gentoo laptop, pyenv, 3.6. Not really env-related.

issue_title: Unsolicited response received on idle HTTP channel starting with "\r\n"; err=<nil>
issue_body:
## Long story short

By default, aiohttp closes keep-alive connections after 75 seconds of inactivity. Just before closing it, server sends `\r\n` data to client in order to notify it about closing. However, not all the clients are happy with that. One of those is standard golang `net/http` which logs errors in this case not about closed connection, but about bad data received in place when there should be any of such.

Referenced commit: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/commit/bf49f8e75fb263113d6a448a532293107d2e03bc

Question is that behaviour correct or common, or based on some RFC? 
What recommendations could we give to clients which complains about to relax their communication with aiohttp servers?

## Expected behaviour

```
2018/03/01 13:52:12 Hello, Anonymous
```

## Actual behaviour

```
2018/03/01 13:52:12 Hello, Anonymous
2018/03/01 13:53:27 Unsolicited response received on idle HTTP channel starting with "\r\n"; err=<nil>
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Run any aiohttp example. Let's take `examples/web_srv.py`
2. Save the following as `main.go`, for example:
```
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "io/ioutil"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "net/url"
    "time"
)

func main() {
    Url, err := url.Parse("http://localhost:8080/hello")
    if err != nil {
        log.Panic(err)
        return
    }

    client := http.Client{
        Timeout: time.Duration(time.Millisecond * 10000),
    }

    resp, err := client.Get(Url.String())
    if err != nil {
        log.Panic(err)
        return
    }

    body, err := ioutil.ReadAll(resp.Body)
    resp.Body.Close()

    log.Print(string(body))
    time.Sleep(100 * time.Second)
}
```
3. Run it as `go run main.go`
4. Wait for awhile and see the error from subject.

## Your environment

1. Python 3.6 / aiohttp 3.0.5
2. go 1.9.1

issue_title: aiohttp is too strict when detecting charset in content_types
issue_body:
## Long story short

The presence of a `;` in a content type does not mean that it contains a charset.

## Expected behaviour

Only complain about charsets if there's a charset in content_type.

## Actual behaviour

`text/plain; version=0.0.4` will raise an `ValueError: charset must not be in content_type argument`.

The problem are these lines: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/4b498d10d1d4672644932d2848d5d4443395f13d/aiohttp/web_response.py#L456-L458

aiohttp just checks for a semicolon instead for the actual word `charset`.

## Steps to reproduce

```pycon
>>> from aiohttp.web import Response

>>> Response(text="hello", content_type="text/plain; version=0.0.4", charset="utf-8")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/Users/hynek/.virtualenvs/prometheus_async/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp/web_response.py", line 451, in __init__
    raise ValueError("charset must not be in content_type "
ValueError: charset must not be in content_type argument
charset must not be in content_type argument
```

issue_title: Cookie retention/handling does not work when using `ClientSession`
issue_body:
## Long story short

Using ClientSession to have aiohttp manage login credentials/session through cookies does not work as expected

## Expected behaviour

When logging in through an aiohttp ClientSession, it is expected to automatically update cookies set by the server and send them again for authentication... This is more or less the number 1 use case for ClientSession (see reproduction below for more details)

## Actual behaviour

ClientSession either does not store the cookies or fails to send them as expected by the server

## Steps to reproduce

Create a server using the code below (run using `fades`, which will help with the dependencies):
```python
#! /usr/bin/fades
from flask import Flask, render_template, request # fades
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy # fades
from flask_security import Security, SQLAlchemyUserDatastore, UserMixin, RoleMixin, login_required, login_user # fades

# Create app
app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['DEBUG'] = True
app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'super-secret'
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:////tmp/testme.sql'

# Create database connection object
db = SQLAlchemy(app)

# Define models
roles_users = db.Table('roles_users',
        db.Column('user_id', db.Integer(), db.ForeignKey('user.id')),
        db.Column('role_id', db.Integer(), db.ForeignKey('role.id')))

class Role(db.Model, RoleMixin):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer(), primary_key=True)
    name = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True)
    description = db.Column(db.String(255))

class User(db.Model, UserMixin):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    email = db.Column(db.String(255), unique=True)
    password = db.Column(db.String(255))
    active = db.Column(db.Boolean())
    confirmed_at = db.Column(db.DateTime())
    roles = db.relationship('Role', secondary=roles_users,
                            backref=db.backref('users', lazy='dynamic'))

# Setup Flask-Security
user_datastore = SQLAlchemyUserDatastore(db, User, Role)
security = Security(app, user_datastore, register_blueprint=False)

# Create a user to test with
@app.before_first_request
def create_user():
    db.create_all()
    user_datastore.create_user(email='test@user.com', password='password')
    db.session.commit()

@app.route('/secret')
@login_required
def secret():
    return 'ok'

@app.route('/login', methods=['POST'])
def login():
    auth_data = request.json
    user = user_datastore.get_user(auth_data['email'])
    login_user(user)
    return 'ok'

if __name__ == '__main__':
    db.drop_all()
    db.create_all()
    app.run()
```

Now create a client using aiohttp with the code below:
```python
#! /usr/bin/env fades
import aiohttp # fades
import asyncio


async def main():
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        async with session.post('http://127.0.0.1:5000/login', json={'email': 'test@user.com', 'password': 'password'}) as response:
            assert response.status == 200

        async with session.get('http://127.0.0.1:5000/secret') as response:
            assert (await response.text()) == 'ok' #<-- this step fails


if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(main())
```

When run together, the second request to `/secret` fails since the serve rejects it.
However, writing an equivalent code in `requests` works as expected:
```python
#! /usr/bin/env fades
import requests # fades

if __name__ == '__main__':
    session = requests.Session()
    session.post('http://127.0.0.1:5000/login', json={'email': 'test@user.com', 'password': 'password'}).raise_for_status()
    resp = session.get('http://127.0.0.1:5000/secret')
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.text == 'ok' #<-- succeeds

```

nobody

It appears that the resolution of tab completion happens asynchronously to the user typing which can results in the import line being garbage.

For example if I type:
```from iter <TAB>``` and then keep typing `tools`, I can end up with:
```
from itertoolstools
```

issue_title: Make ssl an optional import
issue_body:
Not everyone wants to (or can, for license reasons) use openssl.  It looks like we import ssl at https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/a3e074c3a7e29eb3ec8b94627370698458f048ce/notebook/notebookapp.py#L22, but the *only* place the import is used is at https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/a3e074c3a7e29eb3ec8b94627370698458f048ce/notebook/notebookapp.py#L873 and https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/a3e074c3a7e29eb3ec8b94627370698458f048ce/notebook/notebookapp.py#L879, both just for the constants.

Thanks to @williamstein for making us aware of the issue.

issue_title: Enforce a top-down order of execution
issue_body:
Is it possible to force a top-down order for cell execution?  New cells to execute would only be at the bottom of them notebook and existing cells would essentially be immutable.  Maybe if a user wanted to edit or re-execute a cell, a copy could be made at the bottom and that new cell could be executed.

Doing this makes a notebook reproducible. The console has this behavior and its definitely nice being able to know when and what code was run.

issue_title: Continue running notebook output even when tab is closed
issue_body:
Currently a user can log back into a running notebook. However, output is not continually appended.

Since a serialized jupyter notebook contains cell output, it should be possible to keep track of output when a user closes a tab and returns to the notebook by adding to the notebook json as it runs in the background, in which case the output generated while running in the background would be in the notebook.

issue_title: Update PyPI dependency for traitlets > 4.0
issue_body:
I just had a user on a Chromebook using Ubuntu Precise trying to start a Jupyter notebook. Since this system is ARM-based the package selection was more limited and we ended up needing to use miniconda's Python 3.4 and installed Jupyter manually using pip. This failed out of the box with an error that `defaults` could not be imported from traitlets.

This was fixed simply by doing a `pip install --upgrade traitlets` so it looks like the fix could be as simple as having the PyPI release require traitlets>=4.3.

issue_title: [RFC] Tighten valid module version check
issue_body:
Currently, the following module versions will be considered as valid by _pylint-odoo_ when using default configuration:

- `10+0.1.0.0`, because the dot from `valid_odoo_versions` is not escaped when building the valid module version pattern (and the `.` inside a regex matches any character other than a newline): https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L657-L661
- `10.0.1.0.0WHATEVER`, because `re.match` [is used](https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L661) inside `formatversion()`, which [matches the string from the beginning](https://docs.python.org/2/library/re.html#re.match), however, there is no `$` at the end of the default value for `manifest_version_format` to match the end of the string: https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L237
Thus, anything can follow once the version is matched from the beginning of the string.

I have started working on a fix, however, I have stumbled into a slight problem for which I need feedback from the authors - if a _.pylintrc_ file is used with `manifest_version_format` defined inside it using the `%(valid_odoo_versions)s` placeholder, then `manifest_version_format` will be set automatically by `ConfigParser` (which [pylint uses for configuration parsing](https://github.com/PyCQA/pylint/blob/e231a409a83a23c24c8b7dbbaca79314eee6d4c6/pylint/config.py#L462)) due to [interpolation](https://docs.python.org/2/library/configparser.html#ConfigParser.ConfigParser). This means that at the point when the `manifest_version_format_parsed` string is built: https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L657-L661
then `self.config.manifest_version_format` no longer contains the `%(valid_odoo_versions)s` placeholder, as it was already formatted by `ConfigParser`. This becomes a problem if we want to escape the dots from `valid_odoo_versions` (eg. `10.0` -> `10\.0`) inside [`formatversion()`](https://github.com/OCA/pylint-odoo/blob/a14ddca6f0d57e603c55f60b0128b129ad5fb81e/pylint_odoo/checkers/no_modules.py#L657), as our values will be simply ignored if a config file is used with `manifest_version_format` defined inside it using the `%(valid_odoo_versions)s` placeholder.

I have comeup with one solution - switch to [`str.format()`](https://docs.python.org/2/library/stdtypes.html#str.format) style formatting for `manifest_version_format` in the config, which would allow to avoid interpolation by `ConfigParser`. However, I guess it would be a breaking change. So my question is - would it be ok?

Maybe someone has other suggestions?

issue_title: Atlas: Facilitate peer exchange without relying on the TCP socket
issue_body:
Right now, Atlas peers infer the remote requester through the RPC server's TCP socket.  However, this makes it impossible for Atlas peers to discover neighbors when they are behind an HTTP load-balancer or proxy of some kind (i.e. the remote requester will always be 127.0.0.1).

To fix this, we should add a new RPC method called `rpc_atlas_exchange_peers()` that allows the caller to identify itself as a peer when it receives the recipient's list of peers.  This would allow peer exchange to keep working in the event that the node operator does not have a TCP load-balancer handy.

issue_title: Export right-pads CHAR types with extra spaces
issue_body:
In using the `giraffez.Export` module, I've found that CHAR(x) types get padded on the right with a number of spaces equal to the the length of the field (in addition to any blank characters that exist at the end of the value that don't fill the full _x_ characters). I believe expected behavior here would be to trim the value as happens with VARCHAR types, and the additional spaces equal to the length of the field are clearly unwelcome.

Here's an example that I think should be reproducible:

```python
>>> with giraffez.Load(host=host, username=username, password=password) as load:
...     load.execute("""create multiset table giraffez_issue (
...         id_var int,
...         variable_field varchar(10),
...         fixed_field char(4),
...         floaty decimal(6, 2)
...     ) primary index (id_var)""")
...     
...     load.insert('giraffez_issue', [
...         (1, 'alpha', 'A1', 1.1),
...         (2, 'beta', 'B', 2.2),
...         (3, 'gamma', 'C3PO', 3.0)
...     ])
... 

defaultdict(<class 'int'>, {'count': 3})
>>> with giraffez.Export('giraffez_issue', host=host, username=username,
...                      password=password, delimiter='|', null='') as export:
...     for row in export.results():
...         print('<<{row}>>'.format(row=row))
... 
<<1|alpha|A1      |1.10>>
<<3|gamma|C3PO    |3.00>>
<<2|beta|B       |2.20>>
```

I am using the following in pytest-django's tox.ini (via the suggestion in https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-django/pull/412#issuecomment-340077539):

issue_title: Provide a driver_factory - fixture
issue_body:
Having a `driver_factory` fixture additionally to the `driver` factory would be very useful for me (similarly to the pattern of `tmpdir` and `tmpdir_factory`):
* Sometimes I need two seperate browser windows within one test. I couldn't find a way how to do that with the `driver` fixture
* For some of our tests it would be fine, if the browser window is not closed and reopened between running them, in order to save time. However, this requires the fixture to be of a scope of `session`, `module` or `class`

I hacked a modification of the `driver` fixture in my `conftest.py`. Do you think this would be reasonable to include into `pytest-selenium`. If so, I'm happy to create pull request. 

```
@pytest.fixture
def driver_factory(request, driver_class, driver_kwargs):
    """
    Returns a factory function that returns a WebDriver instance when called,
    based on options and capabilities
    """
    request.node.drivers = []

    def factory():
        driver = driver_class(**driver_kwargs)
        request.node.drivers.append(driver)

        event_listener = request.config.getoption('event_listener')
        if event_listener is not None:
            # Import the specified event listener and wrap the driver instance
            mod_name, class_name = event_listener.rsplit('.', 1)
            mod = __import__(mod_name, fromlist=[class_name])
            event_listener = getattr(mod, class_name)
            if not isinstance(driver, EventFiringWebDriver):
                driver = EventFiringWebDriver(driver, event_listener())

        return driver

    yield factory

    for driver in request.node.drivers:
        driver.quit()


@pytest.fixture
def driver(driver_factory):
    return driver_factory()
```

Usage:
```
def test_multiple_windows(driver_factory):
    driver1 = driver_factory()
    driver2 = driver_factory()
    # further test content
```

The possibility to use the `driver_factory` fixture in class-based-fixtures is not given yet, because the `driver_class` and `driver-kwargs` fixtures are `function`-scoped. But I don't see a reason why this couldn't be changed (which would also result in (slightly) faster running tests)

Hi,

I can't work out how to set bounds for CMAESvolutionStrategy.
Could you explain?

Thank you

issue_title: CMA_diagonal /VdK only works with adapt_sigma = FALSE?
issue_body:
Hey,

I was experimenting with CMA-ES with high dimensional parameter spaces.
For speed, I tried to use the CMA_diagonal = TRUE option.
I noticed it only works when I also set adapt_sigma is to FALSE.
Is this normal behaviour?
If so, is there a reason when do not allow sigma to change with a diagonal covariance matrix?
I also noticed that the default setting for adapt_sigma in the VdK sampler is also zero?
What is the advised procedure when using this sampler?
Setting sigma fixed for the entire run, manually restarting the run with a stepwise lower sigma or something else?

Thanks for the nice piece of software and algorithm!
Best Regards

issue_title: operational error: no such table
issue_body:
hello everyone,
I have an error arising when I created new tables in forked partner model. The models work correctly until I use 'ManyToManyField'.
I want to create model in such a way that a new seller can create their profile and select their category(ies).

The error arises when I fill table with the multiple categories.

Please help
Thanks in advance

model.py
from django.db import models
from django_countries.fields import CountryField

class PartnerCategorie(models.Model):
    category = models.CharField(max_length=100)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.category

class RegisterPartner(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    email = models.EmailField()
    contact = models.CharField(max_length=10)
    storeName = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    address1 = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    address2 = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, null=True)
    city = models.CharField(max_length=20, default='New Delhi')
    country = CountryField(default="India")
    pincode = models.CharField(max_length=6)
    GST = models.CharField(max_length=30, null=True)
    PAN = models.CharField(max_length=30, null=True)
    state = models.CharField(max_length=30, default="Delhi")
    Category = models.ManyToManyField(PartnerCategorie)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.storeName

issue_title: Port localhost:5000 not released causing error System.IO.IOException: Failed to bind to address http://127.0.0.1:5000: address already in use.
issue_body:
Hi,

I installed dotnet core on Mac Sierra in order to build .net mvc application & while build a project I learned that donet\kestrel doesn't release the port 5000 after I stop a project causing port already in use error on next run.

I couldn't find any command\manual way to release\stop the port 5000 or localhost:500.

Please enlighten me if any such commend exists or any other possible solution.
error:
`System.IO.IOException: Failed to bind to address http://127.0.0.1:5000: address already in use. ---> Microsoft.AspNetCore.Server.Kestrel.Transport.Abstractions.Internal.AddressInUseException: Error -48 EADDRINUSE address already in use ---> Microsoft.AspNetCore.Server.Kestrel.Transport.Libuv.Internal.Networking.UvException: Error -48 EADDRINUSE address already in use`

Thanks much 👍

issue_title: Build error NU1605 "Detected package downgrade" while adding a netstandart13 library
issue_body:
Given a project which targets netcoreapp2.0. And a package which targets netstandard13 - [Devart.Data.PostgreSql](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Devart.Data.PostgreSql/) (ado.net provider for Postgres).

# Installing in VS
The package cannot be installed in VS2017.3. It silently fails.

# Installing via cli
```
dotnet add package Devart.Data.PostgreSql
```
installs the package, but build fails (it's only a excerpt from full output):
```
D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql>dotnet build
D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.csproj : error NU1605: Detected package downgrade: System.Net.Primitives from 4.3.0 to 4.0.11. Reference the package directly from the project to select a different version. \r [D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.sln]
D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.csproj : error NU1605:  devart_pgsql (>= 1.0.0) -> Devart.Data.PostgreSql (>= 7.9.958) -> NETStandard.Library (>= 1.6.1) -> System.Net.Primitives (>= 4.3.0) \r [D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.sln]
D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.csproj : error NU1605:  devart_pgsql (>= 1.0.0) -> Devart.Data.PostgreSql (>= 7.9.958) -> System.Net.Primitives (>= 4.0.11) [D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.sln]
D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.csproj : error NU1605: Detected package downgrade: System.Reflection from 4.3.0 to 4.1.0. Reference the package directly from the project to select a different version. \r [D:\Work\Learn\dotnet\devart_pgsql\devart_pgsql.sln]
```

Here's the dependencies of the package:
```
    <dependencies>
      <group targetFramework=".NETStandard1.3">
        <dependency id="Microsoft.Extensions.PlatformAbstractions" version="1.0.0" />
        <dependency id="Microsoft.NETCore.Portable.Compatibility" version="1.0.1" />
        <dependency id="Microsoft.Win32.Registry" version="4.0.0" />
        <dependency id="NETStandard.Library" version="1.6.1" />
        <dependency id="System.ComponentModel" version="4.3.0" />
        <dependency id="System.ComponentModel.Annotations" version="4.1.0" />
        <dependency id="System.ComponentModel.Primitives" version="4.1.0" />
        <dependency id="System.ComponentModel.TypeConverter" version="4.1.0" />
        <dependency id="System.Diagnostics.FileVersionInfo" version="4.0.0" />
        <dependency id="System.Net.NameResolution" version="4.0.0" />
        <dependency id="System.Net.Primitives" version="4.0.11" />
        <dependency id="System.Net.Requests" version="4.0.11" />
        <dependency id="System.Reflection" version="4.1.0" />
        <dependency id="System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation" version="4.0.0" />
        <dependency id="System.Security.Cryptography.Algorithms" version="4.2.0" />
        <dependency id="System.Security.Cryptography.Csp" version="4.0.0" />
        <dependency id="System.Threading.ThreadPool" version="4.0.10" />
        <dependency id="System.Xml.XmlDocument" version="4.0.1" />
        <dependency id="Devart.Data" version="5.0.1750" />
      </group>
    </dependencies>
```

I managed to workaround the issue by adding the following package references into csproj:
```
    <PackageReference Include="System.IO.FileSystem.Primitives" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Net.NameResolution" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Net.Primitives" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Reflection" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Security.Cryptography.Algorithms" Version="4.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Security.Cryptography.Csp" Version="4.3.0" />
```

After that the project builds and runs successfully. But only for netcoreapp2.0. 
If I build and run it for net462: 
```
    <TargetFrameworks>netcoreapp2.0;net462</TargetFrameworks>
```
it fails:
```
Error Message:
 System.IO.FileNotFoundException : Could not load file or assembly 'System.Security.Principal.Windows, Version=4.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a' or one of its dependencies. The system cannot find the file specified.
```
So for net462 I added:
```
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net462'">
    <PackageReference Include="System.Security.Principal.Windows" Version="4.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
```
After that it builds and runs on net462 too.

But I think it's too cumbersome. Also it requires manual csproj editing and does not work via VS Package Manager out of the box. So I believe it's not a normal situation.

P.S. I really have no idea where such an issue should be reported, so please feel free to move it to an appropriate repo.

issue_body:
I was trying to migrate my solution of NET CORE 1.1 to NET CORE 2.0 as a linux server.
When I run dotnet xxx.DLL in Ubuntu 14.04 x64 the following problem occurs:

```
Unhandled Exception: System.IO.FileLoadException: Could not load file or assembly 'System.Runtime.Extensions, Version=4.2.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a'. The located assembly's manifest definition does not match the assembly reference. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80131040)
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.WebHostBuilder..ctor()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.WebHost.CreateDefaultBuilder(String[] args)
   at Teste.Program.BuildWebHost(String[] args).
```
Follow some configurations of my project:

**Project.csproj**
```
<TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.0</TargetFramework>
<RuntimeIdentifiers>win10-x64;ubuntu.14.04-x64</RuntimeIdentifiers>
```

I tried to place the missing package in Nuget and the same thing did not work.

Linux server owns NET CORE 2.

Updated VS 2017 (15.3.2)

I checked if the DLL was on the server (and it was).

I tried to place the tag:
 `<AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>`

Does anyone have a suggestion?

**I made a lot of tests simple.** I created a MVC NET CORE 2.0 project and I did not do anything. I compiled for Ubuntu 14.04 and when I try to run it. A similar error occurs:

```
Unhandled Exception: System.IO.FileLoadException: Could not load file or assembly 'System.IO.FileSystem, Version=4.1.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a'. The located assembly's manifest definition does not match the assembly reference. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80131040)
   at Test.Program.BuildWebHost(String[] args)
```

**Is there any problem as the MVC CORE 2 solution in the 14.04 x64 linux server?**

issue_title: Is there a way to get the transition matrix for each input token and class label pairs?
issue_body:
`sklearn-crfsuite` has a `predict_marginals_single` method which given an input sequence `x_1,x_2,..x_n` predicts the marginal probabilities of the class labels `c_1,c_2..`. In other words, it is possible to get the probability (x_k,c_i). I am guessing the marginal for a class is calculated by summing over all the transition probabilities `(c_i,c_j)` at x_k. Is there a way to get the probability for the triple `x_k,c_i,c_j`?

It is possible to get this in Mallet.

issue_title: Python 2 deprecation schedule
issue_body:
I've been giving a lot of thought as to the Python 2 deprecation schedule for `dateutil`, and I think I have come up with a tentative plan. Before I go ahead and put it on [Python 3 statement](https://github.com/python3statement/python3statement.github.io).

Here is my current plan and timeline:

1. August-September 2018: **3.0 release** - Supporting Python 2.7, 3.4+
       We have a lot of deprecated behavior in the 2.x branch that I'd like to remove, and a few small breaks to backwards compatibility that I'd like to make that aren't really something that can be deprecated (e.g. the behavior of `dayfirst` and `yearfirst` that has so many people pinned on 2.4.x). Ideally, we'd also have the new parser up and running before then.
2. January 2019: **4.0 release** - Supporting only Python 3.4+. Version 3.0 becomes LTS, bugfixes only.
    This release should make basically no breaking changes other than dropping support for Python 2, so that people can write Python 2/3 compatible code pinned to `python-dateutil <= 5.0.0`, rather than doing more complicated backports.
3. July 2019 - Final bugfix release for version 3.0. Zoneinfo support continues indefinitely.

After the 4.0 release, I think we'll want to make at least a 6 month deprecation period before any more backwards-compatibility breaking changes are made.

Somewhere in here I'm also planning to put in a compiled backend, but I think that will be opt-in for at least the 2.x branch.

CC @jbrockmendel @jdufresne @jreback

issue_title: Wrong dst() value for time in Portugal tz
issue_body:
Pick a time in the Summer of 1996:
```
>>> t = datetime(1996, 6, 1, tzinfo=tz.gettz('Portugal'))
```
the time zone abbreviation is computed correctly:
```
>>> t.tzname()
'WEST'
```
([WEST](http://www.timeanddate.com/time/zones/west) = Western European Summer Time).

However, 

```
>>> t.dst()
datetime.timedelta(0)
```

and as a consequence:

```
>>> t.timetuple().tm_isdst
0
>>> from time import strftime
>>> strftime('%Z', t.timetuple())
'WET'
```

The problem is that `t.dst()` is computed as a difference between `t.utcoffset()` and the offset of the last standard time before `t`, but Portugal switched to `WEST` in the Spring of 1996 from `CET`, not `WET` and therefore UTC offset did not change, while isdst did.

Below, I've written a minimal program that ideally would run inside a Jupyter Notebook and generate the colored map from http://bl.ocks.org/wrobstory/5609830 . Unfortunately, the map does not render because `_build_map` creates HTML that depends on a `data.json` file (generated in `create_map`, which I never call). 

Instead of requiring File IO in order to create a map that binds features to data (by hard-coding `data.json` as the data path), would you consider adding the option of passing data to `_build_map` and inlining it into the HTML? That way, rendering the result in a Jupyter notebook would be straightforward.

The data files in this example are taken from http://bl.ocks.org/wrobstory/5609830
Example:

```python
import json
import pandas
import folium
from IPython.core.display import HTML

data = pandas.read_csv('tests/us-unemployment.csv')
map = folium.Map(location=[48, -102], zoom_start=3)
geo_path = 'tests/us-states.json'
geo = json.load(open(geo_path, 'r'))
map.geo_json(
    geo_path=json.dumps(geo), 
    data=data, 
    columns=['State', 'Unemployment'], 
    fill_color='YlGn', 
    key_on='feature.id',
)

def inline(m, width, height):
    """Returns an embedded iframe of a folium.map."""
    m._build_map()
    src = m.HTML.replace('"', '"')
    style = "width: {}px; height: {}px".format(width, height)
    return '<iframe srcdoc="{}" style="{}"; border: none"></iframe>'.format(src, style)

HTML(inline(map, 900, 500))
```

Result:

![screen shot 2015-08-13 at 11 04 33 am](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/320145/9257571/2675d204-41ab-11e5-8722-b8ead9cbeecc.png)

issue_title: Feature request: open() methods support for context manager
issue_body:
I found a use case where I'm only interested in managing a pre-configured pyserial object.

```
import contextlib
import serial

# My pre-configured serial object
ser = serial.Serial()
ser.baudrate = 115200

with contexlib.closing(ser.open()):

issue_title: Problem with output layer in neasted model
issue_body:
When I tried to convert yolo v2 model (https://github.com/experiencor/basic-yolo-keras) with mobilenet backend, I got error:

`  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/coremltools/converters/keras/_topology2.py", line 174, in make_output_layers
    self.model.output_layers]
IndexError: list index out of range`


I have debuged code of coreml tool convertor, and I have found that when we loaded backend model in main frontend model, keras  translated it to 3 nested level:
1. Layers of frontend model and backend model layer.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34249234/34248620-db447666-e647-11e7-864a-a57a481cca82.png)

2.  Backend model layer
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34249234/34248667-01a4106e-e648-11e7-9da2-42816801c2fc.png)

3. Layers of backend model
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34249234/34248981-076fa700-e649-11e7-9133-adf44f0c7e93.png)

The problem is related to the fact that output layer of  2-nd model points itself. I change the output layer of 2nd level model on the last layer of 3 level model. 
`
yolo.feature_extractor.feature_extractor.output_layers = [yolo.feature_extractor.feature_extractor.layers[-1].layers[-1]]
`

Now I can convert model without problem.
